name: Ladders were provided by GC on my job site, can I sue them after fall?
description: Yes, for safety failures; third-party suit.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: Ladders were provided by GC on my job site, can I sue them after fall?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, for safety failures; third-party suit.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description:
Yes, general contractors can be liable under scaffold laws for inadequate safety devices like unstable ladders.[1][2]

Separate from comp, sue for full damages if they failed duties.[3]

- Prove improper equipment.
- Owner/GC absolute liability in some states.
- Beyond employer comp.

Consult attorney for third-party claim viability.[2]
